title: Dirge
content:
Gone is he now.
    One flower the less
    Is left to make
    For thee less lone
    Earth's wilderness,
    Where thou
    Must still live on.

    What hath been, ne'er
    May be again.
    Yet oft of old,
    To cheat despair,
    Tales false and fair
    In vain
    Of death were told.

    O vain belief!
    O'erweening dreams!
    Trust not fond hope,
    Nor think that bliss
    Which neither seems,
    Nor is,
    Aught else than grief.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Calverley Trevelyan, titled "Dirge", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
